insight: For Brazil, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.100 per minute, although they did manage to gain momentum in one out of the two breaks. When Brazil was pressing before a break, they struggled to maintain their pace afterwards, losing momentum at a rate of -0.300 per minute, which happened in the one instance where they were pressing. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they actually managed to slightly increase their momentum, gaining at a rate of +0.100 per minute, which could be a useful strategy for them to exploit in future matches.
